refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into sub-functions clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved through measurement, control, and optimization.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify energy consumption data collection accuracy, control actions effectiveness, and optimization algorithm performance against the target energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Measure energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy integrated energy and load sensors with telemetry data aggregation.
DP2: Implement vehicle control algorithms for energy-efficient operation.
DP3: Develop optimization software module for operational parameter tuning.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Deploy integrated energy and load sensors with telemetry data aggregation.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between measurement and control modules
Optimization module requiring accurate measurement data
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports FR1 and provides data for FR2 and FR3; coupling is manageable with clear data interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable energy and load sensors.
Risk of sensor data inaccuracies affecting control and optimization.
Assumes telemetry network supports required data rates and latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing control and optimization DPs separately in next decomposition level to reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical integration of energy and load sensors and local telemetry data aggregation within the vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmit aggregated energy and load data to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Support bidirectional communication for sensor data and control commands.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as sensors are physically on vehicle; low diffusion risk.
Lower containment; would require remote sensing and data aggregation increasing diffusion.
Not suitable for sensor integration; high diffusion risk.
Not related to energy measurement; very high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy measurement sensors and telemetry aggregation are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential integration complexity with vehicle hardware.
Dependency on vehicle maintenance for sensor reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate telemetry interface robustness and sensor calibration procedures in next design iteration.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR1 with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk by containing sensors within Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module provides strong containment for physical sensors and local data aggregation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via sensor calibration tests and telemetry data validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of measurement function to Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose FR2 and FR3 with corresponding DP and IM assignments.
Assess telemetry network capacity and latency impact on real-time control and optimization.

decision.justification: The current level decomposition and assignments for FR1 are sound; next step is to decompose remaining sub-FRs (FR2 and FR3) to complete the energy efficiency KPI coverage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: To refine and decompose remaining sub-functions for complete coverage of the energy efficiency requirement.

assumptions: Reliable sensor technology is available and integrable into Vehicle.
Telemetry network supports required data throughput.
open_risks: Sensor data accuracy and calibration over operational period.
Potential latency in telemetry affecting control and optimization.
evidence_needed: Sensor performance data and calibration procedures.
Telemetry network performance metrics under operational conditions.
